{"key_event":"1979 revolution and birth of Islamic Republic","reasoning":"Oil revenues, SAVAK, the military buildup, and the single-party state made the Shah’s state extremely strong by the mid-1970s, but mass strikes, nationwide demonstrations, army neutrality, and revolutionary upheaval shattered the Pahlavi apparatus; the Islamic Republic was rebuilding authority by late 1979 but remained less consolidated than the old monarchy. Civil society rose sharply through urbanization, mosque-bazaar networks, workers’ strikes, students, clerics, and revolutionary mobilization, though pluralist forces were already being narrowed by clerical consolidation and the hostage crisis.","society_change":3.0,"society_power":6.5,"state_change":-2.4,"state_power":5.8}
